commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bfac33c88f679c48aff24c52fede2035edeede3a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `maintenances.isEmpty` to `BlacksmithStatus.isOperational`, adds a maintenance-only status model test, and updates `CHANGELOG.md`.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection and the contributor's model output show current main returns operational and `All systems operational` for `UP` with no incidents and one maintenance entry; the PR regression for scheduled maintenance is source-reproducible because the new predicate ignores `StatusEvent.status`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the branch still needs status-aware maintenance semantics and contributor-supplied real behavior proof; ClawSweeper repair should not be requested while the proof gate is mock-only.

target.title: Treat maintenance-only window as not operational
target.body:
### What

`BlacksmithStatus.isOperational` only checked `pageStatus == "UP"` and `incidents.isEmpty`, ignoring active maintenances:

```swift
var isOperational: Bool {
    pageStatus.uppercased() == "UP" && incidents.isEmpty
}
```

So for a maintenance-only window (UP, no incidents, one active maintenance) `isOperational` returns `true`, even though every other computed property treats a maintenance as an active notice:

- `hasActiveNotice` -> `true` (it includes `!maintenances.isEmpty`)
- `badgeLabel` -> `"MAINT"`
- `noticeKind` -> `"Maintenance"`
- `noticeTitle` -> the maintenance name

The visible result is that `label` short-circuits to `"All systems operational"` while the badge next to it shows `"MAINT"`.

### Fix

Include `maintenances.isEmpty` in `isOperational` so it agrees with `hasActiveNotice`, and `label` falls through to the maintenance name:

```swift
pageStatus.uppercased() == "UP" && incidents.isEmpty && maintenances.isEmpty
```

### Test

Added a case to `StatusModelTests` for a maintenance-only window: asserts `isOperational == false` and `label` is the maintenance name. It fails before the change (`isOperational -> true`, `label -> "All systems operational"`) and passes after. Full suite green (23 tests).



Update: added real behavior proof in a comment below - runtime main-vs-fix table for the maintenance-only window plus `swift test` output.
target.author: devyrpauli
